eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: PanelSpriteButton
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: A button that can be customized.
description: The button can be customized with a background for each state and a label. It handles user interactions and a simple condition can be used to check if it is clicked.
There are ready-to-use buttons in the asset-store [menu buttons pack](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?initial-dialog=asset-store&asset-pack=menu-buttons-menu-buttons).

changelog.breaking: - Buttons now use "variants", allowing easy swapping of their visual aspect. You will have to make some adjustments to existing buttons in your project. Follow this [migration documentation](https://wiki.gdevelop.io/gdevelop5/objects/custom-objects-prefab-template/migrate-to-variants/) to do these changes.

Make sure the cursor position is only checked once per frame.
Touches are always pressed, so ShouldCheckHovering doesn't matter.

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: PanelSpriteSlider
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: A draggable slider that users can move to select a numerical value.
description: A draggable slider that users can move to select a numerical value. The slider can be customized with sprites.
There are ready-to-use sliders in the asset-store [settings UI pack](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?initial-dialog=asset-store&asset-pack=settings-ui-settings-ui).

changelog.breaking: - Sliders now use "variants", allowing easy swapping of their visual aspect. You will have to make some adjustments to existing sliders in your project. Follow this [migration documentation](https://wiki.gdevelop.io/gdevelop5/objects/custom-objects-prefab-template/migrate-to-variants/) to do these changes.

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: TwoChoicesDialogBoxes
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: A dialog box with buttons to let users make a choice.
description: A dialog box showing multiple options (usually "yes" and "no") and a customizable text message.
It handles keyboard, gamepad and touch controls.

eventsBasedObjects.description: A dialog box showing two options.
eventsBasedObjects.name: TwoChoicesDialogBox

events.comment:
Gamepad, mouse, touch and keyboard are fully supported.
Each action from a device increment or decrement an index that is used to focus the active button, make outlines and trigger a sound effect.
To escape the dialog we can focus the button with the identifier 1 which is usually the button to cancel/deny by pressing one of the key or buttons

eventsFunctions.description: Check if the "Yes" button of the dialog was selected.
eventsFunctions.name: IsYesClicked

events.comment:
The button is clicked
Or
The button is active and a key is pressed to validate the button

eventsFunctions.description: Check if the "No" button of the dialog was selected.
eventsFunctions.name: IsNoClicked

events.comment:
The button is clicked
Or
The button is active and a key is pressed to validate the button

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: jack, DHsoftware, @Bouh
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: Iframe
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Create or delete an iframe to embed websites.
description: This extension allows you to set up and create Iframes to show websites on your game.
For security reasons on browsers, some websites can be limited if the host and the iFrame do not have the same origin.

eventsFunctions.description: Create a new Iframe to embed a website inside the game.
eventsFunctions.name: Create
eventsFunctions.sentence: Create Iframe _PARAM1_ at position _PARAM5_:_PARAM6_, width: _PARAM3_, height: _PARAM4_, url: _PARAM2_

eventsFunctions.description: Delete the specified Iframe.
eventsFunctions.name: Delete

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: Gabriel (@TheGemDev)
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: InternetConnectivity
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Checks if the device running the game is connected to the internet.
eventsFunctionsExtensions.description: Checks if the device running the game is connected to the internet.

eventsFunctions.description: Checks if the device is connected to the internet.
eventsFunctions.name: IsDeviceOnline

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: LoadImageFromURL
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Adds multiple actions to load images from a URL into the game.
description: With this extension, you can load images from any URL (including a DataURL) into a sprite or image resource.

Loading it into a sprite will load the URL into a sprite, replacing the image it displays until the sprite's displayed image is changed, e.g. by going to the next frame of an animation or switching animations.

Loading it into a resource will discard the old image that a resource was using and replace it with the image loaded from a URL. Any sprite that is displaying this resource as a part of their animation will start showing the new image instead of the  old one. The old image of the resource will not be accessible anymore unless you restart the game or reload the original image from a URL into it.

eventsFunctions.description: Replaces the image contained by a sprite by a new one, from a URL. This will only affect the sprite in question and only until the image in it is changed through its animation or another action, unless you also modify the resource.
eventsFunctions.name: LoadURLIntoSprite
eventsFunctions.sentence: Load URL _PARAM1_ into sprite _PARAM2_ (and into the corresponding resource: _PARAM3_)

inlineCode: for (const obj of objects) obj.getRendererObject().texture = texture;

parameters.description: The URL to load the new image for the sprite from
parameters.name: URL

parameters.longDescription: If yes, modifies the image contained in the resource of the sprite's current frame instead of just the sprite's displayed image. This makes the changes affect all other sprites that also display this resource, and allows the change to persist after changing animations or the current frame.
parameters.name: ChangeResource

eventsFunctions.description: Replaces the image contained by a resource by a new one, from a URL. This will update all sprites displaying the resource.
eventsFunctions.fullName: Load URL into an image resource
eventsFunctions.name: LoadURLIntoImageResource

parameters.description: The URL to load the new image for the resource from
parameters.name: URL

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: Arthur Pacaud (arthuro555)
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: References
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Adds references and pointers for variables.
eventsFunctionsExtensions.description: Adds actions to use references (variable that redirect any read/write to another variable) and pointers (unique numbers identifying a variable that can be resolved to a reference). Some usages are for callback variables in events based functions (getting a variable with a fixed name referring to a variable with a name passed as parameter), pointers to make advanced data structures like linked lists, and getting a top level variable dynamically.

eventsFunctions.description: Transforms a scene variable into a reference to another scene variable.
eventsFunctions.name: CreateReference

parameters.description: The scene variable to turn into a reference
parameters.name: reference

parameters.description: The scene variable the reference will refer to
parameters.name: source

eventsFunctions.description: Transforms a scene variable into a reference to a global variable.
eventsFunctions.name: CreateGlobalReference

parameters.description: The scene variable to turn into a reference
parameters.name: reference

parameters.description: The global variable the reference will refer to
parameters.name: source

eventsFunctions.description: Transforms a scene variable into a reference to an object's variable.
eventsFunctions.name: CreateObjectReference
eventsFunctions.sentence: Make variable _PARAM2_ a reference to variable _PARAM3_ of _PARAM1_

parameters.description: The object that possesses the variable to refer to
parameters.name: Object

parameters.description: The scene variable to turn into a reference
parameters.name: reference

parameters.description: The object variable the reference will refer to
parameters.name: source
